engineGuide.h2: Ford Ka Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Ford Ka across both MK1 (1996-2008) and MK2 (2008-2016) generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: The 1.3L Endura-E Rocam engine powered the majority of MK1 Ford Ka models, using a pushrod OHV design that prioritised simplicity and low-cost ownership.
compatibleVariants: Ka 1.3, Ka Collection, Ka Grand Prix, Ka Style, Ka Tattoo, Ka Zetec, Ka Base, Ka Trend
commonFailures: Timing belt failure (interference engine - belt snap destroys valves and pistons)
Head gasket failure on high-mileage examples (60,000+ miles)
entries.cta: Get quotes for A9B Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: An evolution of the Endura-E Rocam family, the A9D appeared in later MK1 models with minor emissions calibration changes for Euro compliance.
compatibleVariants: Ka 1.3, Ka Collection, Ka Zetec, Ka Style
commonFailures: Timing belt failure (interference design - catastrophic engine damage)
Coolant loss from failed water pump
entries.cta: Get quotes for A9D Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The entry-level 1.0L Endura-E engine offered the most affordable Ka ownership experience, though its low power output made it less popular for replacement searches than the 1.3L units.
commonFailures: Oil leaks from rocker cover gasket
entries.cta: Get quotes for AUA Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: A later variant of the 1.0L Endura-E, the AUB code appeared on MK1 models with updated emissions components for Euro 3 compliance.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AUB Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.0L Petrol
entries.history: The final 1.0L Endura-E variant, produced for late MK1 models before the MK2 generation introduced the Duratec engine family.
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals
entries.cta: Get quotes for AUC Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: An early Endura-E Rocam variant fitted to first-generation MK1 Ka models, identifiable by earlier emissions certification and component differences.
commonFailures: Carburettor/injection system issues on early models
entries.cta: Get quotes for J4C Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: A transitional 1.3L Endura-E variant produced for early-to-mid MK1 production, bridging early and later engine specifications.
entries.cta: Get quotes for J4D Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Petrol
entries.history: A less common 1.3L Endura-E engine code found on some European-market MK1 Ka models, producing slightly lower power output than the dominant A9B.
commonFailures: Valve seat wear on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for BAA Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The dominant MK2 Ka petrol engine, the 1.2L Duratec 8V brought modern chain-driven reliability and improved fuel economy to the second-generation Ka.
compatibleVariants: Ka 1.2, Ka Studio, Ka Zetec, Ka Style, Ka Style+, Ka Collection, Ka TDCi (petrol variant), Ka Grand Prix
commonFailures: Valve seat recession on higher-mileage units
entries.cta: Get quotes for A4B Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: A higher-compression variant of the Duratec 1.2L, the A4C offered marginally improved power output for later MK2 Ka models.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A4C Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: A late-production Duratec 1.2L variant fitted to final-year MK2 Ka models before discontinuation.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A4D Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: A lower-volume Duratec 1.2L engine code found on specific MK2 Ka trim levels with emissions calibration differences.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A4E Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.2L Petrol
entries.history: The final Duratec 1.2L variant code, produced for the last MK2 Ka models before the Ka+ replaced the standard Ka in the UK market.
entries.cta: Get quotes for A4F Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: The performance heart of the SportKa, the 1.6L Zetec-SE delivered 95hp and transformed the city car into a genuine hot hatch rival, though SportKa engines are now rare in the used market.
commonFailures: Coil pack failure causing misfires above 4,000rpm
Oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals
entries.cta: Get quotes for M7A Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: An early Zetec-SE variant fitted to the first SportKa models, producing the same 95hp output as the later M7A.
entries.cta: Get quotes for KN Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Petrol
entries.history: A mid-production Zetec-SE variant for the SportKa, carrying the same performance characteristics as earlier and later 1.6L units.
entries.cta: Get quotes for LQ Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: The dominant MK2 Ka diesel engine, the 1.3L Duratorq TDCi was sought after by high-mileage commuters for its economy, though EGR and oil dilution issues made replacement common.
compatibleVariants: Ka 1.3 TDCi, Ka Zetec TDCi, Ka Studio TDCi, Ka Style TDCi
commonFailures: Oil dilution from failed DPF regenerations
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9J Ford Ka engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L Diesel
entries.history: A lower-volume Duratorq TDCi variant, the F9Q shares most mechanical components with the F9J but with different emissions calibration for specific UK market requirements.
entries.cta: Get quotes for F9Q Ford Ka engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Ford Ka Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.h4: Ford Ka Timing Belt Failure - Why Endura-E Engines Snap Belts & Destroy Themselves
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 40,000-70,000 miles or 5-8 years (interval often ignored on budget cars)
problems.rootCause: All Ka Endura-E engines are interference designs - when the rubber timing belt snaps, valves collide with pistons causing bent valves, damaged pistons, and often cracked cylinder heads. Owners skip recommended 5-year/50,000-mile belt changes due to cost, leading to catastrophic engine destruction.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Timing Belt Replacement (belt only, before failure)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New timing belt kit, tensioner, water pump (recommended)
repairOptions.longevity: 5 years / 50,000 miles - only viable before belt snaps

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Used Engine (belt snapped, engine destroyed)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit used Endura-E engine, no warranty on internal condition
repairOptions.longevity: Unknown - buyer beware on used units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt engine with new belt, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 5+ years / 50,000+ miles - the financially sensible option

problems.recommendation: On a MK1 Ford Ka worth £500-£1,500, a preventative belt change (£200-£350 specialist) is excellent value if caught early. However, once the belt has snapped, the engine is almost always beyond economical repair. A rebuilt Endura-E with 12-month warranty (£800-£1,400 supply & fit) costs less than a main dealer replacement and gives you warranty cover - something a used engine never provides.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Endura-E engine prices for your Ford Ka MK1

problems.group: Ford Ka 1.2 Duratec Timing Chain Stretch
problems.h4: Ford Ka 1.2 Duratec Timing Chain Stretch - The MK2's Silent Engine Killer
problems.rootCause: Unlike the belt-driven Endura-E, the 1.2 Duratec uses a timing chain. However, the chain tensioner wears and the chain stretches over time, eventually causing timing drift, poor running, and in severe cases, valve contact with pistons. Extended oil change intervals accelerate wear.

repairOptions.tier: Preventative - Timing Chain Replacement (before catastrophic failure)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New timing chain kit, tensioner, guides, sprockets

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Used Engine (chain snapped or timing jumped)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit used 1.2 Duratec engine, no internal warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Unknown - high risk on chain-stretched units

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded chain tensioner, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles - peace of mind for daily driver

problems.recommendation: If your 1.2 Duratec Ka has developed a rattle on cold start or the engine management light is flashing timing correlation codes, replace the chain preventatively (£400-£650 specialist). This is cheaper than scrapping a MK2 Ka worth £800-£2,000. If the chain has already jumped, a rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner is the only safe option - used engines often have the same design flaw waiting to fail.
problems.cta: Get quotes for A4B Duratec engine replacement for your MK2 Ka

problems.group: Ford Ka 1.3 TDCi EGR Valve Clogging
problems.h4: Ford Ka 1.3 TDCi EGR Valve Clogging - Diesel Ka's Most Common Failure
problems.rootCause: The 1.3 TDCi's EGR valve recirculates exhaust gases back into the intake, causing carbon buildup that sticks the valve open or closed. Symptoms include rough idle, loss of power, black smoke, and eventually limp mode. On high-mileage cars, clogging can lead to oil dilution and turbo damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Manual carbon removal from EGR valve and intake ports
repairOptions.longevity: 20,000-30,000 miles - temporary fix on high-carbon engines

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New OEM EGR valve fitted, system reset

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine (if oil dilution caused bearing damage)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 1.3 TDCi with new EGR, turbo inspection, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles - only when engine internals already damaged

problems.recommendation: Start with EGR cleaning (£100-£180) if the car has been used for short urban trips. If the problem returns within 6-12 months, replace the EGR valve (£200-£350). However, if the car has been driven for months with EGR issues causing oil dilution (rising oil level on dipstick), the bottom end bearings may already be damaged. In this case, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.3 TDCi engine prices for your Ford Ka diesel

problems.h4: Ford Ka Coil Pack Failure - The Petrol Misfire That Leads to Catalytic Converter Damage
problems.affectedModels: Ford Ka MK1 1.0/1.3 Endura-E (all petrol codes) and MK2 1.2 Duratec (A4B/A4C/A4D) - all petrol Ka variants
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 50,000-90,000 miles (accelerated by heat cycling and age)
problems.rootCause: Ford Ka petrol engines use individual coil packs (or a single coil pack on early MK1) that degrade over time. A failing coil pack causes misfires, unburnt fuel entering the exhaust, and overheating of the catalytic converter. Left undiagnosed, the catalytic converter fails, adding £300-£600 to repair costs.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Single Coil Pack Replacement (identify which cylinder)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnose misfiring cylinder, replace faulty coil pack

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Coil Pack Set Replacement (preventative)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all coil packs, spark plugs recommended
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000+ miles - best value for high-mileage cars

repairOptions.tier: Major - Coils + Catalytic Converter (if cat damaged by misfire)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace all coils, spark plugs, and damaged catalytic converter
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000+ miles - expensive but cheaper than replacement

problems.recommendation: If your Ka is misfiring and the engine light is flashing, replace the failing coil pack immediately (£80-£150). Driving even 50 miles with a flashing light can destroy the catalytic converter. On a high-mileage Ka (80,000+ miles), proactively replacing all four coils and spark plugs (£180-£280) is cheaper than replacing a melted cat later.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Ford Ka Duratec and Endura-E coil pack replacement

problems.h4: Ford Ka Head Gasket Failure - Overheating on High-Mileage Endura-E Engines
problems.affectedModels: Ford Ka MK1 1.3 Endura-E (A9B/A9D/J4C/J4D) - 1996-2008, higher risk on 80,000+ mile examples
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000-100,000 miles (often triggered by coolant leaks or failed thermostats)
problems.rootCause: The Endura-E's cast iron block and aluminium cylinder head expand at different rates. Over time, the head gasket loses its seal, especially after overheating events caused by failed water pumps, stuck thermostats, or coolant leaks. Symptoms include mayonnaise-like oil, coolant loss, overheating, and white smoke from exhaust.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Head Gasket Replacement (without head damage)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New head gasket, head skim if warped, coolant flush, new thermostat

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Used Engine (cracked head or warped beyond skimming)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit used Endura-E engine, no internal warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Unknown - risk of repeating same failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with new head gasket, pressure-tested head, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 50,000+ miles - best value for keeper cars

problems.recommendation: On a MK1 Ka worth £500-£1,200, a head gasket replacement (£450-£700) makes sense only if the car is otherwise clean and the head hasn't warped. If the engine has overheated severely causing a cracked head, or the car has other rust/structural issues, a rebuilt engine or scrapping the car are your only options. For owners committed to keeping their Ka on the road, a rebuilt unit with warranty (£800-£1,400) costs less than replacing the car.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 1.3 Endura-E engine prices for your MK1 Ford Ka

problems.group: Ford Ka SportKa 1.6 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Ford Ka SportKa 1.6 Timing Chain Failure - The Performance Model's Weak Point
problems.rootCause: The SportKa's 1.6 Zetec-SE uses the same timing chain design as other Ford Zetec-SE engines, with a tensioner that fails and a chain that stretches over time. Symptoms include rattling on cold start, poor acceleration, and eventually timing jump causing valve contact with pistons. SportKa owners often drive harder, accelerating chain wear.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New timing chain kit, tensioner, guides, sprockets, water pump

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Used Engine (chain snapped, valves bent)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit used 1.6 Zetec-SE from breaker
repairOptions.longevity: Unknown - SportKa engines are rare and often high-mileage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit fully rebuilt unit with upgraded chain components, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 60,000+ miles - the only reliable option for rare SportKa

problems.recommendation: SportKa values are rising (£1,500-£4,000 for good examples), making preventative chain replacement (£450-£700 specialist) excellent value. If the chain has already failed, avoid used engines - SportKa units are hard to find and often have the same tensioner flaw. A rebuilt 1.6 Zetec-SE with upgraded chain components (£1,100-£1,800 supply & fit) preserves the car's value and gives you warranty protection.
problems.cta: Get quotes for SportKa 1.6 Zetec-SE engine replacement

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Ford Ka
finalCta.paragraph: Timing belt snaps, stretched timing chains, clogged EGR valves, and failed coil packs are the most common reasons Ford Ka engines are scrapped prematurely. But replacement doesn't have to mean sending your Ka to the breaker. EnginesMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Ford Ka specialists - whether you need a rebuilt Endura-E for your MK1, a Duratec for your MK2, or a 1.3 TDCi for your diesel commuter. Every engine comes with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, upgraded components designed to address the original failure point, and nationwide delivery or supply & fit options. Compare prices today and get your Ka back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Ford Ka Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EnginesMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Ford Ka and engine code. Every engine supplied through EnginesMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Ford Ka Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant

items.title: Ford Ka 1.3L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.3L Duratorq TDCi unit was the only diesel engine offered in the Ford Ka, available exclusively on the MK2 generation from 2008 to 2016. Based on the Fiat Multijet architecture shared with the second-generation Ka's Fiat 500 platform, this engine appealed to high-mileage commuters and fleet buyers seeking exceptional fuel economy. Though economical, the 1.3 TDCi developed a reputation for emissions system problems that often drove replacement decisions.
commonFailurePoints: oil dilution from failed DPF regenerations
items.cta: Get quotes for Ford Ka 1.3L diesel engines

items.title: Ford Ka 1.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The entry-level 1.0L Endura-E Rocam engine powered base-spec MK1 Ka models from 1996 to 2008, offering the lowest running costs but also the least power in the range. This simple pushrod OHV design prioritised affordability and ease of repair over performance, making it a common fitment in first-car and urban-only Ka examples. Though less frequently searched for replacement than the 1.3L, failure rates remain significant due to timing belt neglect on budget-conscious ownership cycles.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure (interference engine - catastrophic damage)
items.cta: Compare Ford Ka 1.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Ka 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L Duratec 8V engine was the dominant petrol unit across the entire MK2 Ka range from 2008 to 2016, replacing the Endura-E family with modern chain-driven reliability and improved fuel economy. Shared with the Fiat 500, this engine became the most common replacement search term for second-generation Ka owners due to high UK registration volumes. Though more refined than the MK1's pushrod units, the 1.2 Duratec introduced timing chain stretch as its signature failure mode.
commonFailurePoints: valve seat recession on high-mileage units
items.cta: Compare Ford Ka 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Ka 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L Endura-E Rocam engine was the best-selling petrol unit across the entire MK1 Ka production run from 1996 to 2008, offering a genuine 60hp and far more torque than the 1.0L entry option. This pushrod OHV design became known for its simplicity and low running costs, but its interference timing belt meant catastrophic failure was common when owners skipped recommended changes. The 1.3L remains the most searched MK1 petrol displacement for engine replacement queries.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt failure (belt snap destroys valves
head gasket failure on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Ford Ka 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Ford Ka 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L Zetec-SE engine was fitted exclusively to the SportKa performance variant from 2003 to 2008, transforming the MK1 city car into a genuine hot hatch with 95hp and significantly sharper handling. This engine shared its architecture with the Ford Fiesta ST of the same era, making it the most powerful factory option ever offered in the Ka. Though SportKa numbers were limited, replacement demand remains strong from enthusiasts preserving rare examples, with timing chain failure being the most common replacement driver. Not sure which engine size your Ford Ka needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EnginesMarket include a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Ford Ka specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch/tensioner failure from 50,000-80,000 miles
coil pack failure causing misfires above 4,000rpm
oil sludge buildup from extended service intervals
items.cta: Compare Ford Ka 1.6L petrol engine prices

"New Edge" design language debuted on a production car
Ka and Ka2 trims offered initially (Ka3 added 1999)

knownFor: Surprisingly spacious front cabin despite tiny footprint
OHV engine design dated back to 1960s Kent unit
Fun handling became the Ka's trademark
Simple mechanics make rebuilds straightforward

Brazilian 1.0L flex-fuel introduced but not UK market

SportKa transformed the Ka into a genuine hot hatch
Timing belt failure remains critical on interference engines
StreetKa rare two-seat convertible now a cult classic

faq.h2: Ford Ka Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What's the most reliable Ford Ka engine to buy?
items.answer: The MK1 1.3 Duratec petrol (A9B/A9D) is widely considered the most reliable Ka engine, regularly reaching 120,000-180,000 miles with basic maintenance. Avoid the MK2 1.3 TDCi diesel if reliability is your priority.
keyPoints: MK1 1.3 Duratec petrol regularly exceeds 120,000-180,000 miles with basic maintenance
MK2 1.2 Duratec is solid but timing chain may stretch from 60,000 miles
MK2 1.3 TDCi diesel has known EGR and DPF issues, especially on short trips
SportKa 1.6 Zetec-SE is fun but chain tensioner needs monitoring from 50,000 miles
items.cta: Compare Ford Ka 1.3 Duratec engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How much does a Ford Ka engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Ford Ka engine costs £500-£1,400 supply only, depending on the engine code. Add £250-£500 for supply and fit. Used engines start from £350 but come with minimal warranty.
keyPoints: MK2 1.2 Duratec rebuilt: £500-£1,100 supply only
MK1 1.3 Duratec rebuilt: £450-£950 supply only
1.3 TDCi diesel rebuilt: £650-£1,400 supply only
SportKa 1.6 rebuilt: £650-£1,250 supply only
Supply and fit adds approximately £250-£500 to any engine price
items.cta: Get instant Ford Ka engine replacement quotes for your specific engine code

items.question: What are the most common Ford Ka engine problems?
items.answer: Timing belt failure on MK1 1.3 Endura-E engines is catastrophic - belt snaps destroy valves and pistons. On MK2 1.2 Duratec, timing chain stretch from 60,000 miles is the main issue. Diesel Ka EGR valve clogging is also very common.
keyPoints: MK1 Endura-E timing belt: interference design, belt snap destroys the engine
MK2 Duratec timing chain: stretches from 60,000 miles causing rattles and poor running
1.3 TDCi EGR valve: clogs from 40,000 miles, causing rough idle and power loss
Coil pack failure: common on both MK1 and MK2 petrol variants
Rocker cover gasket leaks: minor but common across all Ka petrol engines
items.cta: Compare Ford Ka engine repair costs vs replacement prices

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Ford Ka?
items.answer: Yes, if the car is otherwise solid. A rebuilt Ka engine costs £500-£1,400 fitted. With a clean MK1 Ka worth £1,000-£2,000 and MK2 worth £1,000-£3,000, replacement often makes more financial sense than scrapping.
keyPoints: Ka values have stabilised - clean examples are holding their price
A rebuilt engine with 12-month warranty costs less than replacing the car
MK1 rust and MK2 diesel DPF issues aside, most Kas are mechanically simple to repair
Used engine £350-£750 fitted offers the lowest entry point
Rebuilt engine with upgraded components is recommended if keeping the car long-term
items.cta: Find out if your Ford Ka qualifies for cost-effective engine replacement

items.question: Ford Ka timing belt or chain - which do I have?
items.answer: MK1 Ford Ka (1996-2008) uses a timing belt on all engines - replace every 5 years/50,000 miles. MK2 (2008-2016) uses a timing chain on both petrol and diesel - chain rarely needs replacement but tensioners can fail.
keyPoints: MK1 all engines: timing belt (interference design) - failure destroys engine
MK2 1.2 Duratec: timing chain - listen for rattle on cold start from 60,000 miles
MK2 1.3 TDCi diesel: timing chain - generally reliable but tensioner can fail
SportKa 1.6: timing chain - known tensioner weakness, check from 50,000 miles
MK1 belt replacement typically £200-£350 at a specialist
items.cta: Get a Ford Ka timing belt or chain replacement quote from UK specialists

items.question: What engine oil does my Ford Ka need? And how often should I change it?
items.answer: Most Ford Ka engines use 5W30 fully synthetic oil. Change annually or every 6,000-10,000 miles - the official 12,500-mile service interval is too long for older, high-mileage engines .
keyPoints: 1.3 Endura-E (MK1): 5W30, 3.5 litres, change every 6,000 miles for longevity
1.2 Duratec (MK2): 5W30, 3.2 litres, change annually or 8,000 miles
1.3 TDCi diesel: 5W30 low-ash, critical for DPF health - do not extend intervals
Small sump means oil degrades faster - check level monthly
Regular oil changes prevent timing chain stretch on MK2 Duratec engines
items.cta: Check your Ford Ka's exact oil specification and book a service

items.question: Ford Ka 1.2 Duratec timing chain problems - what do I need to know?
items.answer: The MK2 Ka 1.2 Duratec's timing chain can stretch from 60,000 miles, causing rattle on cold start and eventually timing drift. If the chain jumps, valves hit pistons. Replace the chain preventatively if you hear rattling.
keyPoints: Chain stretch affects A4B, A4C, A4D codes most commonly
Cold start rattle is the first warning sign
Ignoring chain noise leads to timing jump and bent valves
Preventative chain replacement: £400-£650 at specialist
Full engine replacement after chain failure: £900-£1,500
items.cta: Get Ford Ka 1.2 Duratec timing chain replacement quotes

items.question: Ford Ka 1.3 TDCi diesel EGR valve problems - how to fix?
items.answer: EGR valve clogging is the most common diesel Ka problem, causing rough idle, power loss, and black smoke from 40,000 miles . Cleaning (£100-£180) works temporarily; replacement (£200-£350) is the permanent fix.
keyPoints: Symptoms: rough idle, fluctuating revs, loss of power, black smoke on acceleration
Urban driving accelerates carbon buildup in the EGR valve
Cleaning works for 6-12 months; replacement is longer-term solution
If oil level is rising on dipstick, oil dilution has occurred - engine damage may already be present
Extended oil changes make EGR problems worse - use correct low-ash oil
items.cta: Compare Ford Ka 1.3 TDCi EGR repair vs engine replacement costs

items.question: What's the difference between MK1 and MK2 Ford Ka engines? Can I swap between generations?
items.answer: MK1 (1996-2008) uses Ford's own Endura-E engines with timing belts. MK2 (2008-2016) uses Fiat-derived Duratec engines with timing chains. They are not interchangeable - completely different engine mounts, wiring looms, and ECUs.
keyPoints: MK1 engines: 1.0/1.3 Endura-E belt-driven, simple OHV design
MK2 engines: 1.2 Duratec chain-driven, Fiat 500 architecture
MK2 diesel: 1.3 TDCi Fiat Multijet - not available in MK1 at all
Engine mounts, gearbox bolt patterns, and electronics are generation-specific
Stick to same engine code when replacing - A4B for A4B, A9B for A9B
items.cta: Need the right engine for your MK1 or MK2 Ford Ka? Get matched with compatible suppliers

items.question: Should I buy a used, rebuilt, or reconditioned Ford Ka engine?
items.answer: Used (£350-£750) is cheapest but riskiest - no internal warranty. Rebuilt (£500-£1,100) restores reliability with new gaskets and bearings. Reconditioned (£800-£1,500) returns to factory specification with all wear parts replaced. For long-term ownership, rebuilt or reconditioned is better value.
keyPoints: Used engine: lowest cost, but condition of timing components is unknown
Rebuilt engine: new gaskets, rings, bearings - timing components inspected
Reconditioned engine: machined to factory tolerances, hot-tested - best warranty
All EnginesMarket engines include 12-month minimum warranty
Avoid engines without documented history - especially on high-failure codes like the 1.2 Duratec
items.cta: Compare used, rebuilt and reconditioned Ford Ka engine prices side by side
